title: Technology decisions for business apps, portals, and internal tools | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: Technology decisions for business apps, portals, and internal tools...
seo.description: Overview of technology pages covering Next.js, TypeScript, API integrations, and PostgreSQL in the context of business software and internal tools.

page.title: Technology decisions for business apps, portals, and internal tools | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Technology decision pages for business applications and internal systems
page.description: Overview of technology pages covering Next.js, TypeScript, API integrations, and PostgreSQL in the context of business software and internal tools.
page.primaryQuery: Technology decision pages for business applications and internal systems

hero.title: Technology explained through project fit rather than trend chasing
hero.subtitle: Useful when a buyer or team needs to understand why a stack choice or integration approach is commercially sensible for the project at hand.

intro: These pages are not a tool catalogue. They are decision content for the moment when a team needs to justify a stack or integration direction in business terms.
The section covers Next.js, TypeScript, API integrations, and PostgreSQL in the context of business applications, portals, and internal systems.

sections.title: What this section covers
body: Each child page explains where the technology is strong, what to watch out for, and which type of business project benefits most from it.

sections.title: Who this section helps most
body: This section works as a supporting authority layer for buyers and teams that need better decisions around technology, templates, or local commercial context.
bullets: buyers who need technology translated into business terms
internal teams validating an architecture direction
projects focused on integrations, maintainability, or long-term stack choices

sections.title: How to continue from here
body: Open the child page closest to the current decision and then continue into the relevant service, comparison, or inquiry path.
bullets: open the technology page tied to the current decision
follow into the relevant comparison or service page
move to inquiry if you need advice grounded in your actual project context

faq.question: Are these pages only for technical readers?
faq.answer: No. They are written to help commercial and delivery stakeholders understand stack decisions too.

faq.question: Do they connect back to services?
faq.answer: Yes. Every technology page links into the commercial context where it is most relevant.

faq.question: Are they useful in inherited-app situations?
faq.answer: Yes. They can help frame whether the current stack still supports the product well.

for: buyers who need technology translated into business terms
internal teams validating an architecture direction
projects focused on integrations, maintainability, or long-term stack choices
notFor: reading with no connection to a real software decision

cta.label: Discuss your project
cta.note: If this section connects to a live project, I can help frame the next step against your context.
